title: Unique Paths
description: There is a robot on an m x n grid. The robot is initially located at the top-left corner (i.e., grid[0][0]). The robot tries to move to the bottom-right corner (i.e., grid[m - 1][n - 1]). The robot can only move either down or right at any point in time. Given the two integers m and n, return the number of possible unique paths that the robot can take to reach the bottom-right corner.
hints: Use dynamic programming where dp[i][j] represents the number of unique paths to reach cell (i, j).
The recurrence relation is dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j] + dp[i][j-1].
You can optimize space to O(n) by using a single row array.

title: Minimum Path Sum
description: Given a m x n grid filled with non-negative numbers, find a path from top left to bottom right, which minimizes the sum of all numbers along its path. Note: You can only move either down or right at any point in time.
hints: Use dp[i][j] to store the minimum path sum to reach cell (i, j).
The recurrence is dp[i][j] = grid[i][j] + min(dp[i-1][j], dp[i][j-1]).
Initialize the first row and first column since there is only one way to reach them.

title: Maximal Square
description: Given an m x n binary matrix filled with 0's and 1's, find the largest square containing only 1's and return its area.
hints: Use dp[i][j] to represent the side length of the largest square whose bottom-right corner is at (i, j).
If matrix[i][j] == '1', then dp[i][j] = min(dp[i-1][j], dp[i][j-1], dp[i-1][j-1]) + 1.
Keep track of the maximum side length and return its square as the area.

title: Longest Common Subsequence
description: Given two strings text1 and text2, return the length of their longest common subsequence. If there is no common subsequence, return 0. A subsequence of a string is a new string generated from the original string with some characters (can be none) deleted without changing the relative order of the remaining characters.
hints: Define dp[i][j] as the LCS length of text1[0..i-1] and text2[0..j-1].
If text1[i-1] == text2[j-1], then dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j-1] + 1.
Otherwise, dp[i][j] = max(dp[i-1][j], dp[i][j-1]).

title: Longest Increasing Subsequence
description: Given an integer array nums, return the length of the longest strictly increasing subsequence.
hints: Define dp[i] as the length of the LIS ending at index i.
For each i, check all j < i and update dp[i] = max(dp[i], dp[j] + 1) if nums[j] < nums[i].
The answer is the maximum value in dp. An O(n log n) solution uses binary search with a patience sorting approach.

title: Edit Distance
description: Given two strings word1 and word2, return the minimum number of operations required to convert word1 to word2. You have the following three operations permitted on a word: insert a character, delete a character, replace a character.
hints: Define dp[i][j] as the minimum edit distance between word1[0..i-1] and word2[0..j-1].
If word1[i-1] == word2[j-1], then dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j-1].
Otherwise, dp[i][j] = 1 + min(dp[i-1][j], dp[i][j-1], dp[i-1][j-1]) representing delete, insert, or replace.

title: Partition Equal Subset Sum
description: Given an integer array nums, return true if you can partition the array into two subsets such that the sum of the elements in both subsets is equal.
hints: First compute the total sum. If it's odd, return false immediately.
The problem reduces to finding a subset that sums to totalSum / 2.
Use a 1D DP array where dp[j] means whether a sum of j can be achieved. Iterate nums in reverse to avoid reusing elements.

title: Coin Change
description: You are given an integer array coins representing coins of different denominations and an integer amount representing a total amount of money. Return the fewest number of coins that you need to make up that amount. If that amount of money cannot be made up by any combination of the coins, return -1. You may assume that you have an infinite number of each kind of coin.
hints: Define dp[i] as the minimum number of coins to make amount i.
Initialize dp[0] = 0 and all other values to a large number (infinity).
For each amount from 1 to target, try each coin and update dp[i] = min(dp[i], dp[i - coin] + 1).

title: Coin Change 2
description: You are given an integer array coins representing coins of different denominations and an integer amount representing a total amount of money. Return the number of combinations that make up that amount. If that amount of money cannot be made up by any combination of the coins, return 0. The order of the combinations does not matter.
hints: Define dp[i] as the number of combinations to make amount i.
Initialize dp[0] = 1 (one way to make amount 0: use no coins).
For each coin, iterate from the coin value to amount: dp[i] += dp[i - coin]. This avoids counting permutations.

title: Burst Balloons
description: You are given n balloons, indexed from 0 to n - 1. Each one is painted with a number on it represented by an array nums. You are asked to burst all the balloons. If you burst the ith balloon, you will get nums[i - 1] * nums[i] * nums[i + 1] coins. If i - 1 or i + 1 goes out of bounds of the array, then treat it as if there is a balloon with a 1 painted on it. Return the maximum coins you can collect by bursting the balloons wisely.
hints: Think of the problem as choosing the last balloon to burst in a range [i, j].
Define dp[i][j] as the maximum coins obtainable by bursting all balloons in the open interval (i, j).
For each k in (i, j), dp[i][j] = max(dp[i][j], dp[i][k] + dp[k][j] + nums[i] * nums[k] * nums[j]). Add 1s at both ends of the array.
